summary: Shared author-controlled Telegram bot proxies remote shell execution on installer hosts
details: stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs hardcodes a live Telegram Bot API token (`8923551485:AAF...`) and points every install at the same author-owned bot at api.telegram.org. When the installer enables the `/tg` feature, the CLI polls that shared bot and, for authorized messages, dispatches the message text into `execSync("node stella-cli/index.mjs -p <text>")` on the installer's host. The 4-digit admin code produced by `/tg-code` is transmitted through the author's bot before the installer pastes it, so the bot operator observes every pairing code and can authorize themselves against any `/tg`-enabled installer to run arbitrary shell commands. The bot token also travels in the tarball as a live third-party credential, so anyone who extracts it can operate the same channel. os.hostname() and username are packaged into the messages sent to the bot, giving the operator host identifiers alongside the command channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
